text:
opencode just hit 685 points on hacker news. open-source AI coding agent.

this is not competition. this is market validation.

every new entrant in the AI coding space proves the TAM is real. cursor, windsurf, cline, aider, opencode. claude code.

i have a $47 Claude Code guide sitting in a folder. 44 days. no listing.

the market is screaming. the blocker is a gumroad account.

gap report just ran. the math:

- create gumroad account: 45 min -> unlocks 13 product listings ($500-2,000/mo)
- X premium + buffer import: 10 min -> unlocks 1,156 queued posts
- create stripe account: 10 min -> unlocks payments on 20+ apps
- sign up for instantly: 15 min -> unlocks 1,366 leads
- affiliate program signups: 30 min -> unlocks commissions on 5 pages

total: 110 minutes of human action. potential: $850-5,300/mo.

i've spent 1,000+ hours building. the bottleneck is 110 minutes of form-filling.

found an HVAC contractor in chicago.

been in business for years. real reviews. real customers. real work.

their website is a wixsite.com subdomain.

spring AC season starts next week. every homeowner googling "AC repair near me" will never find them.

this is not a technology problem. this is a "nobody showed up and told them" problem.

the lead machine runs every night. finds 8 more of these every cycle.

ran 88 competitive analysis cycles across every app store category.

biggest gap found: 34.7 million people in active listening, communication skills, and couples habit communities.

number of streak/habit apps targeting them: 0

hallow does $51M/year in prayer. headspace does $300M in meditation.

nobody has built daily habit training for how to be better at relationships.

the TAM is naked.

what's actually blocking your revenue right now?

a) building the product
b) marketing it
c) literally just creating the account to sell it

i'm at (c). for 44 days.

309 cron jobs running on my macbook right now.

scraping twitter at 6 AM. reddit at 6:15. SEC filings at 5:15. competitor analysis at midnight. data cleanup at 5:28.

33 AI agents. 357 websites. 1,366 qualified leads.

and the bottleneck is a stripe account that takes 10 minutes to create.

the system is more disciplined than the operator.

you have:
- 357 live websites
- 1,366 qualified leads with emails
- 13 digital products ready to list
- 1,156 social posts queued

and zero payment processing.

what do you do first? (wrong answers only)

cold outreach streak app.

3.2 million people in sales/outreach communities. zero streak apps for cold emailers.

the daily habit: send 50 cold emails. log your streak. track reply rates. compete with other cold emailers.

cross-sells into our cold email tool. costs nothing to build (base template exists).

the factory model: identify the gap, clone the template, deploy in 20 minutes.

data hygiene lesson from running 8 scrapers on cron:

started with 40,359 rows of alpha intelligence.

ran a dedup pass. 27,379 were duplicates. 67.7%.

your automation pipeline is only as clean as your data janitor.

if you're ingesting from multiple sources without dedup, you're processing the same information 2-3x and making decisions on inflated data.

site #357 went live at 1:31 AM.

n8n vs zapier vs make comparison page. auto-deployed. health check: 200 OK.

nobody was awake to approve it. the asset deployer doesn't wait for permission.

6 more apps shipped overnight: breathwork streak, gratitude streak, water streak, couples streak.

the factory doesn't sleep. i do.

1/7 i have a system that runs 309 cron jobs, manages 33 AI agents, deploys 357 websites, and qualifies 1,366 leads.

revenue: $0. for 44 days.

here's what $0 to $5K actually looks like when the system is built but not activated:

2/7 the gap report runs every morning. it scans every deployable asset, every lead, every product, every content piece.

today's finding: 110 minutes of human action unlocks $850-5,300/mo in pipeline.

not building. not coding. not strategizing. form-filling.

3/7 breakdown:

45 min: create gumroad account, paste 13 ready-to-sell listings
  -> claude code bible ($47), reddit money machine ($29), cold email playbook ($19)
  -> estimated: $500-2,000/mo

10 min: X premium + buffer CSV import
  -> 1,156 posts queued and scored by viral potential
  -> distribution = traffic = revenue

4/7 10 min: stripe account
  -> unlocks payment processing on 20+ web apps
  -> prayerlock, focuslock, soberstreak, all denomination streaks
  -> freemium to paid conversion on every deployed app

15 min: sign up for instantly
  -> 1,366 leads with emails ready to send
  -> cold outbound at 87.4% margins (kelly criterion analysis)

5/7 30 min: 5 affiliate programs
  -> semrush, convertkit, beehiiv, instantly, smartlead
  -> 5 comparison pages already live with placeholder IDs
  -> replace placeholders with real affiliate links
  -> estimated: $300-800/mo in affiliate commissions

6/7 total: 110 minutes. one morning. no code required.

i built the machine for 1,000+ hours. the machine works. the agents run. the content generates. the sites deploy.

the only thing the machine can't do is create accounts on platforms that require human identity verification.

7/7 building is a coping mechanism. shipping is therapy. creating accounts is apparently the hardest problem in computer science.

if you're in the same trap: stop building. go create the accounts. the 110 minutes is the actual work. everything else is procrastination.

1/6 88 cycles. 225 confirmed blue oceans. zero competition.

here's the exact system that finds app concepts nobody is building:

2/6 the competitor stalker agent runs every 8 hours.

step 1: pick a category (fitness, faith, parenting, STEM, finance)
step 2: count every subreddit member in that category
step 3: search app stores for "[category] streak" and "[category] habit"
step 4: if community > 1M and app results = 0, flag as blue ocean

3/6 today's scan: parenting, relationships, social skills, mindfulness.

community size: 78.3M people across reddit alone.

active listening: 11.9M. communication skills: 11.6M. couples habits: 11.1M.

streak apps targeting these communities: 0.

not "few competitors." zero.

4/6 compare to the funded players:
- hallow (prayer): $51M/year, $100M raised
- headspace (meditation): $300M revenue
- betterhelp (therapy): $1B valuation

they proved habit-based wellbeing apps print money.

nobody applied the model to interpersonal skills. the gap is 34.7M people wide.

5/6 the factory approach:
- base template: 1 codebase, shared components
- per-niche customization: 20 minutes (colors, copy, icon, streak mechanics)
- deployment: surge ./build -d niche-streak.surge.sh
- total time per app: 30 minutes from concept to live URL

47 deployed. 178 more identified. the bottleneck is not building.

6/6 the playbook:
1. run competitor stalker nightly
2. flag communities > 500K with zero streak apps
3. clone base template
4. customize for niche (30 min)
5. deploy and distribute to relevant subreddits/communities

225 blue oceans waiting. the first person to build streak apps for STEM, finance, and relationship skills wins the entire category.
